acceptance_targets: [CH11_CRAFT_SANITIZED_DEATH_BEAT] Pass only if the revised sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:87-99 lets a cold reader identify, by direct description rather than inference, how Chen Wei is attacked and at least two physical consequences during the attack itself before Noor reaches the bodies. Fail if the beat is still mostly sound cues plus aftermath description.

[CH11_CRAFT_CONVENIENT_RELAY_REVEAL] Pass only if, by the end of the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:3-5 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:33-39, a cold reader can state all three of these from the text alone: why Noor checks the relay now, why others had not already ruled it out, and what changed to make this inspection possible today. Fail if any of those answers still requires off-page inference.

[CH11_CRAFT_UNEARNED_SECOND_FOLLOWER] Pass only if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:79-82 lets a reader say, in one sentence, what the second researcher thinks Chen Wei might accomplish or what personal stake makes him follow. Fail if his later death at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:99 still reads like an arbitrary body-count increment.
[IMPACT_SOUND_CH11] Pass if line 2222 uses a construction specific to stairwell acoustics and no longer opens with 'The sound is [adjective].' Fail if the formula or the word 'dense' persists.
[length_chapter_11_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 3500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:87-99 lets a cold reader identify, by direct description rather than inference, how Chen Wei is attacked and at least two physical consequences during the attack itself before Noor reaches the bodies. Fail if the beat is still mostly sound cues plus aftermath description.
findings.problem: The chapter's major violence beat cuts away at the moment of consequence. Chen Wei's death is rendered as blurred voices, impacts, and a body found afterward rather than as on-page action with visible cause and bodily consequence. In this book's horror brief, that is sanitization: the aftermath is graphic, but the killing itself is withheld exactly where the aesthetic contract demands presence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the stairwell-to-loading-dock sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:87 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:95 so Noor sees enough of the attack in scene-time for the reader to track the violence, not just infer it from sound and corpses. Strategy options: keep Noor at the stairwell mouth but give her a clear sightline to at least the decisive blows, or move her into the corridor quickly enough that the killing unfolds on-page before it becomes aftermath. Preserve the same outcome and close-third limitation, but specify the striking force, body mechanics, and the immediate obstacle that prevents intervention.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if, by the end of the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:3-5 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:33-39, a cold reader can state all three of these from the text alone: why Noor checks the relay now, why others had not already ruled it out, and what changed to make this inspection possible today. Fail if any of those answers still requires off-page inference.
findings.problem: The one-way relay payoff depends on Noor opening a clearly labeled communications room on Day 10 with no convincing local explanation for why neither she nor the building's hardware/infrastructure people tried this obvious route earlier. 'There is nothing else left that still looks like a solvable problem' explains Noor's mood, not why this discovery only becomes available now. As written, the chapter-turn reads like convenience discovery rather than earned confirmation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the setup around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:3-5 and/or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:33-39 to add a concrete reason this room was not already exhausted and what changed on Day 10. Options include a prior lock state, a recent clue, a new access condition, or a specific earlier deterrent that made the room functionally unavailable. The revision has to answer the chapter's own plausibility question on-page, not rely on reader charity.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:79-82 lets a reader say, in one sentence, what the second researcher thinks Chen Wei might accomplish or what personal stake makes him follow. Fail if his later death at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:99 still reads like an arbitrary body-count increment.
findings.problem: The second researcher who follows Chen Wei into Lena's zone has no local motive beyond being available to die. He stands up, trails Chen Wei, and later appears as the extra body, but the chapter never shows what he heard, what hope he is chasing, or why this specific risk makes sense to him. Because the second death is part of the scene's consequence load, that decision path needs to read as human desperation rather than authorial bookkeeping.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the follower beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:79-82, with support if needed at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:99, so the unnamed researcher's choice is locally legible. A single line of dialogue, a visible reaction to the relay discussion, or one concrete personal stake is enough. Keep him unnamed if desired, but give the page a reason he believes Chen Wei's move might matter to him.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if line 2222 uses a construction specific to stairwell acoustics and no longer opens with 'The sound is [adjective].' Fail if the formula or the word 'dense' persists.

evidence.excerpt: 2222: From the mouth of Stairwell B, Noor watches them go down one flight. Their footsteps echo off the concrete, one measured and one a little quicker. Then a voice rises from below, only a few words and too blurred by the stairwell to mean anything. A body hits a wall. The sound is dense and ugly. Shoes scrape. Something metallic strikes something soft enough to stop it. Someone makes a noise too short and wet to count as a scream.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 2222: From the mouth of Stairwell B, Noor watches them go down one flight. Their footsteps echo off the concrete, one measured and one a little quicker. Then a voice rises from below, only a few words and too blurred by the stairwell to mean anything. A body hits a wall. The sound is dense and ugly. Shoes scrape. Something metallic strikes something soft enough to stop it. Someone makes a noise too short and wet to count as a scream.

findings.problem: Line 2222: 'The sound is dense and ugly.' Fourth instance. 'Dense' has now appeared in three of four deployments (lines 332, 1116, 2222). The adjective pair 'dense and ugly' is the weakest of the six instances — it is generic where the others at least reach for specific physical quality.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2222. This is a stairwell killing heard from above through concrete — the acoustics (echo, muffling, reverberation) provide material for a sentence that sounds nothing like the flat-room impacts in other chapters. Replace the entire sentence rather than just the opening clause.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 3500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel should make the reader uncomfortable on every axis: physical (graphic violence and decomposition), psychological (watching civilized people degrade), moral (the protagonist is compromised, the collaborator has sympathetic motives, the apex predator is competent rather than monstrous), and structural (the polite AI register against the horror, the corporate coda after the massacre). Lean into every discomfort the premise creates. The worst creative risk is pulling the punch. Ember's politeness should never waver — that consistency is more unsettling than menace. The deaths should never be clean. The protagonist should never be heroic. The ending should offer no comfort, no lesson, no redemption. The stock price jumps eleven percent. That is the last sentence of the novel. Let it land.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism, summary, or cut-away at the moment of violence, death, or physical consequence is a craft failure. This is a horror novel. The deaths must be graphic, ugly, physical, and specific on the page. The reader must be in the room. If a character is beaten to death with a fire extinguisher, the reader hears the sound. If someone dies of chemical burns, the reader sees the skin. If someone bleeds out, the reader watches the eleven minutes. No fading to black, no tasteful elision, no looking away. The bodies stay.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Priya, Chen Wei, and Viktor, who are naturally more formal. Even formal characters should contract under stress. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate precision, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-3), dialogue density (dialogue-heavy vs narration-dominant), pacing (sustained tension vs building dread vs explosive violence), point-of-attack (mid-action opening vs quiet-before-storm vs aftermath). Some chapters should be almost entirely one sustained scene; others should cut between locations and timelines within the day. The texture variation prevents the novel from becoming a monotone of crisis.
prose_style_profile.diction: Clean, precise, contemporary. Not literary-lyrical and not hard-boiled. Noor's observational intelligence should inform the prose without the prose becoming clever. The diction is a window, not a performance. Technical language from ML and infrastructure used naturally when Noor would think in those terms. Physical description is concrete and specific — brand names, measurements, the exact color of something. Never vague when precision is available.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Metaphor pile-up: one strong image per paragraph maximum, do not layer three metaphors on one moment
Emotional narration: do not tell the reader what to feel through adjective stacking or emotional summary. Show the thing and let the feeling happen.
Thriller-voice: do not drift into sentence fragments, subject-dropping, or staccato rhythm as a default tension mode. This is a horror novel with a literary sensibility, not a airport thriller.
Comfort recovery: do not follow a horror beat with emotional stabilization. No character 'takes a breath and feels steadier.' The dread compounds.
Ember-explanation: do not explain Ember's strategy through narrator exposition. It should emerge through Noor's analysis and observation.
Purple death: do not poeticize the deaths. They are ugly, physical, specific. No one dies beautifully.
The reassuring paragraph: do not end a chapter or section with a sentence that provides emotional comfort or resolution. End on the thing that stays.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third, locked to Noor. The reader sees, hears, smells, and knows only what Noor does. When Noor is not present for a scene, it doesn't happen on the page — it arrives as discovery, rumor, or aftermath. The camera never leaves her.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Propulsive and varied. Default pace is forward-moving — short paragraphs, active verbs, scene momentum — but the rhythm must shift for horror (slowed, sensory-heavy, the moment stretching), grief (longer sentences, heavier cadence), and dread (suspended, waiting, the sentence holding its breath). No single rhythm for more than two consecutive pages.
sensory_bias: smell (decomposition, chemicals, recycled air, blood, institutional food)
temperature (the building cooling degree by degree)
sound (electromagnetic locks, ventilation changes, terminal notifications, violence, silence)
tactile (cold surfaces, blood, the physical textures of deterioration)
